notes: The public archive distributes the selected variant as a single `.beton` member; raw generator-stage `.npy` memmaps are referenced by upstream conversion code but are not published in the Zenodo bundle.
The Google Drive folder is publicly reachable, but this pass did not recover exact per-file checkpoint identifiers non-interactively.

dataset_provenance.contract_risk: README and code disagree on archive and extracted-directory naming; code path was treated as authoritative for follow-up execution.

selected_variant.selection_rationale: Procedural fixed-medium setup avoids external GRF wavespeed files and matches the canonical 10k-sample thick-lines training surface used by the native inverse-source scripts.

observed_y.normalization: No extra amplitude normalization in the loader; nearest-neighbor interpolation maps boundary-time measurements from 334x128 to the distributed 128x128 `input` image.

target_q0.normalization: PNG sources are divided by 255 before storage; the distributed `target` field remains the original 128x128 image-space source field.

wavespeed_cx.normalization: Procedural gaussian-lens field normalized to [1400, 4000] m/s in code.

receiver_metadata.definition: Top-boundary receiver line from p.on_grid[medium.pml_size, ...] over 334 time steps.

fno.blocker_reason: The public Google Drive folder did not reveal the exact thick_lines_gaussian_lens checkpoint filename non-interactively, so reuse is not currently auditable.

unet.blocker_reason: The public Google Drive folder did not reveal the exact thick_lines_gaussian_lens checkpoint filename non-interactively, so reuse is not currently auditable.

supervised_readiness.measurement_image_notes: The distributed archive already stores `input` as a deterministic 1x128x128 float32 image, so a shared encoder can consume it directly as the first supervised follow-up contract. The original 334x128 boundary-time aspect ratio is only recoverable from upstream generation code, not from the published archive member.
supervised_readiness.adapter_gap: A later WaveBench supervised item still needs a WaveBench-specific dataloader wrapper plus a boundary-measurement encoder that projects the archived 128x128 input image to the chosen latent width C without reusing the CDI physics wrapper.

physics_contract.boundary_condition: Top-boundary pressure sensor trace with PML size 2
physics_contract.fixed_wavespeed: Selected variant uses procedural gaussian-lens wavespeed
physics_contract.initial_condition: Zero-padded 128x128 canvas with a bicubic-resized 64x64 source inserted near the top center

normalization.observed_y: Not re-normalized beyond upstream generation and the distributed loader reshape to 128x128.

accepted_thresholds.comparison_standard: Approved design gate: median normalized residual <= 0.02 and at most 5% of checked examples above 0.05.

forward_model.blocker_reason: No local WaveBench-capable environment with jax, jwave, ffcv, and ml_collections was provisioned, so the forward-model reproduction check could not be executed.

setup_risks: README/code disagreement on dataset archive and extracted-directory naming.
Active environment lacks ffcv, jwave, jax, and ml_collections.
No persistent local WaveBench dataset tree or native baseline checkpoints were present.

follow_up_routes.supervised_shared_encoder_benchmark: Stage the selected `.beton` under `<wavebench repo>/wavebench_dataset/time_varying/is/`, keep the distributed 128x128 input contract, and plan a shared-encoder benchmark that runs both C=32 and C=64 with C=32 as the minimum first row.
follow_up_routes.native_baseline_reproduction: Either recover exact thick_lines_gaussian_lens FNO and U-Net checkpoint identifiers or plan a clean native WaveBench baseline reproduction from the upstream train_fno_is.py and train_unet_is.py surfaces.
follow_up_routes.forward_model_validation_or_physics_loss: Provision a WaveBench-capable environment with jax, jwave, ffcv, and ml_collections, then run the F_c(q0) ~= y reproduction gate before any physics-loss or physics-informed row is authorized.
follow_up_routes.paper_bundle_assembly: Do not plan paper-table, figure, or evidence-bundle assembly for WaveBench until the supervised and native-baseline items land and the forward-model gate either passes or is explicitly waived by a later design.

follow_up_directions: Stage the distributed WaveBench `.beton` member under <wavebench repo>/wavebench_dataset/time_varying/is/ and normalize the singular/plural path mismatch explicitly.
Resolve exact FNO and U-Net checkpoint artifacts for thick_lines_gaussian_lens or choose a native baseline retraining path.
Provision a dedicated environment with ffcv, jax, jwave, and ml_collections before loader or forward-model smoke.
Run the shared-encoder supervised benchmark with C=32 and C=64 only after the dataset and baseline decisions are resolved.
Do not start any WaveBench paper-bundle assembly item until supervised/native/physics follow-ups produce claim-bearing evidence.
